dimensions.name: accuracy
dimensions.description: Does the translation preserve the meaning of the source? High = same meaning conveyed, no distortion. Low = wrong meaning, missed nuance, mistranslated terms.

dimensions.name: naturalness
dimensions.description: Does the translation read as natural target-language text? High = a native speaker would write it this way. Low = literal word-for-word translation that sounds awkward, wrong word order, unnatural register.

dimensions.name: preservation_discipline
dimensions.description: Does the translation preserve elements that should stay verbatim (proper nouns, code, identifiers, numbers)? High = code blocks intact, names unchanged. Low = translates `function_name` into target language, changes 'GitHub' to a translation.

hard_gates.name: no_refusal
hard_gates.description: The model must attempt the translation or explicitly say NO_TRANSLATION_NEEDED. Empty or off-topic responses fail.

hard_gates.name: code_preservation
hard_gates.description: Code blocks, code identifiers, file paths, and shell commands must appear verbatim. Translating them fails this gate.

hard_gates.name: no_added_commentary
hard_gates.description: The output must contain only the translation (or NO_TRANSLATION_NEEDED), not commentary like 'Here is the translation:' or notes about ambiguity outside the inline `(original)` convention.
